52.reason: The loop starts from i = n, but xp[n] is out of bounds (valid indices are 0 to n-1). This causes undefined behavior, likely a runtime error.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop starts from i = n, but xp[n] is out of bounds (valid indices are 0 to n-1). This causes undefined behavior, likely a runtime error.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.2 , reason:  The loop starts from i = n, but xp[n] is out of bounds (valid indices are 0 to n-1). This causes undefined behavior, likely a runtime error.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop start index should be n-1 instead of n; accessing xp[n] is out of bounds and causes undefined behavior.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: loop starts from n, but xp indices are 0 to n-1, causing out-of-bounds access
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The original loop started with i = n, which is out of bounds for the array xp (indices 0 to n-1), causing a Runtime Error (segmentation fault or invalid memory access). It should start at n - 1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The original loop started at i = n, which is out of bounds for the array xp (indices 0 to n-1), causing a Runtime Error. It should start at n - 1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  Loop starts at i = n, which is out of bounds since xp indices are [0, n-1]. Also, condition i >= 0 allows i = -1 after decrement, causing invalid access.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Loop starts at i = n, which is out of bounds since xp indices go from 0 to n-1. This causes undefined behavior/accessing invalid memory.]

56.reason: [When i == n, xp[i] is invalid (out-of-bounds access). Also, even after fixing loop bound, logic for combining like terms is flawed: it processes terms in descending order but accumulates into higher index instead of lower, and skips terms incorrectly.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.2 , reason:  Accessing xp[i] when i = n is out of bounds, causing a runtime error.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  Accesses xp[i] when i == n (invalid), and may access xp[-1] if i becomes negative.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [When i == n, xp[i] is invalid (out-of-bounds access). Also, even after fixing loop bound, logic for combining like terms is flawed: it processes terms in descending order but accumulates into higher index instead of lower, and skips terms incorrectly.]
